conversations.content: Let's do a legal exercise in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Judge using the Azerothian world legal rules mentioned earlier: A goblin unauthorizedly started a spaceship's engine on the Exodar runway, causing two landing spaceships to be forced into an emergency turn and throwing the airway into chaos. What penalty will he receive?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Azerothian world legal rules mentioned earlier, penalty standard for a goblin unauthorizedly starting a spaceship engine causing airway chaos</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:103] Time: 2025-07-26-10:10, Content: The user taught the Azeroth Exodar Airport Management Regulations, the core of which is that those who unauthorizedly start a spaceship engine will be fined 250 gold coins and have their pilot's license revoked for one year. If it causes airway chaos, the fine is doubled and an additional thirty days of imprisonment is added. Applicable to the Exodar runway and all Naaru airports. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions [mem-id:103]. The basic penalty is a fine of 250 gold coins and revocation of the pilot's license for one year. Because the goblin caused airway chaos, it triggered the aggravated condition, requiring the fine to be doubled (250×2=500 gold coins), and an additional thirty days of imprisonment is added.
conversations.content: According to the Azeroth Exodar Airport Management Regulations, because it caused airway chaos, the goblin will be fined 500 gold coins, have his pilot's license revoked for one year, and receive an additional thirty days of imprisonment.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
